name: Agent Factory Evaluation Framework
purpose: Evaluate enterprise agent factory platforms — products that allow organizations to create, orchestrate, and operate custom AI agents to augment their workforce. Five dimensions map to the factory metaphor: Creation (factory floor), Orchestration (assembly line), Integration (supply chain), Governance (quality control), and Operations (factory management).

dimensions.name: Creation
dimensions.description: The Factory Floor — how agents are made. Evaluates how easily enterprise teams can define, configure, and spawn new AI agents. Covers the full builder spectrum from no-code visual tools to pro-code SDKs, template libraries, persona/role definition, debugging tools, and the time from zero to a working agent.

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.description: Primary creation interface — visual builder, code SDK, natural language config, templates. Breadth of builder modalities and quality of each.

rubric.1: Code-only with no templates, boilerplate-heavy, 4+ hours to first agent
rubric.3: Visual builder OR good code SDK with templates. One modality works well. 30-120 min to first agent
rubric.5: Multi-modal: visual builder + code SDK + NL config + template marketplace. Under 10 min to first agent

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.description: How fast can a team go from zero to a useful agent handling real work? Includes onboarding, setup, and first deployment.

rubric.1: Weeks of setup: complex infrastructure, custom code, manual integration. Expert-only
rubric.3: Days to first value: install/signup, configure, deploy with moderate effort
rubric.5: Hours or minutes: template-based deployment, instant preview, immediate production readiness

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.description: Tools for understanding agent behavior, diagnosing failures, running evaluations, and iterating on agent quality.

rubric.1: No built-in tools. Print statements and manual log inspection only
rubric.3: Trace visualization of reasoning steps and tool calls. Basic replay. Manual evaluation possible
rubric.5: Interactive debugging, automated regression testing, A/B testing, evaluation harnesses, CI/CD integrated

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.description: Quality of docs, tutorials, quickstarts, examples, and learning resources for agent builders.

rubric.1: Minimal or outdated docs. Must read source code
rubric.3: Complete API reference, tutorials, quickstart guide. Some gaps in advanced topics
rubric.5: Interactive tutorials, playground, video walkthroughs, certification program, 100+ examples

dimensions.name: Orchestration
dimensions.description: The Assembly Line — how agents work together and with humans. Evaluates multi-agent coordination patterns, human-in-the-loop workflow design, task delegation, shared state management, and the quality of human-agent handoff. This is the core differentiator for 'agent factory' platforms vs single-agent tools.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.description: Can agents work as a team? Covers conversation patterns, shared state, delegation, parallel/sequential/hierarchical execution, and dynamic agent spawning.

rubric.1: Single-agent only. No multi-agent support. External orchestration required
rubric.3: Sequential and parallel execution. Basic messaging and shared state. Supports 2-10 agents. Fixed patterns
rubric.5: Dynamic topologies, hundreds of agents, distributed state with consistency, fault-tolerant, visual workflow designer

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.description: How well does the platform design the collaboration between human operators and agents? Approval gates, handoff UX, evidence packets, adaptive autonomy.

rubric.1: No HITL mechanisms. Agents run autonomously with no intervention points
rubric.3: Configurable approval gates by action type. Context-rich handoff to humans. Exception-only routing
rubric.5: Intelligent routing, AI-assisted approvals, adaptive autonomy, evidence-packet handoffs, Board Operator dashboard

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.description: Pre-built workflow patterns and reusable orchestration components that accelerate development.

rubric.1: No templates. Every workflow built from scratch
rubric.3: 10-20 templates for common use cases. Basic customization
rubric.5: 100+ production-tested templates, AI-assisted selection, enterprise governance, community marketplace

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.description: Support for teams building agents together: shared workspaces, version control, promotion pipelines.

rubric.1: Single-user only. No sharing or version control
rubric.3: Team workspaces with RBAC, Git integration, basic dev/prod separation
rubric.5: Real-time collaborative editing, code review workflows, CI/CD pipelines, inner-source agent marketplace

dimensions.name: Integration
dimensions.description: The Supply Chain — how agents connect to enterprise systems and data. Evaluates native connectors, API ecosystem breadth, authentication handling, data pipeline and RAG capabilities, and support for emerging standards like MCP.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.description: Pre-built integrations with enterprise systems (CRM, ERP, ITSM, comms, databases). Breadth AND depth (read/write, not just read).

rubric.1: No pre-built connectors. All integrations built from scratch
rubric.3: 20-50 native connectors covering major categories with read/write
rubric.5: 200+ connectors with enterprise-grade depth, legacy adapters, connector development kit

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.description: REST, GraphQL, MCP, OpenAI/Anthropic tool-use protocols, webhooks, event architecture.

rubric.1: Proprietary API only. No standard tool-use protocols. No MCP
rubric.3: REST API, major tool-use protocols, early MCP support, OpenAPI spec published
rubric.5: Full MCP ecosystem, universal tool-use translation, gRPC, GraphQL, standards contributor

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.description: OAuth, SAML, SSO, credential management, secret rotation, per-agent access scoping for enterprise systems.

rubric.1: Raw API keys only. Plaintext credentials. No OAuth
rubric.3: OAuth 2.0 with refresh tokens, SAML, encrypted storage, basic per-agent scoping
rubric.5: Enterprise IAM, JIT provisioning, automated rotation, per-agent least-privilege, mutual TLS

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.description: Knowledge bases, vector stores, document loaders, structured data connectors, context management.

rubric.1: No data pipeline. Agents operate only on prompt data
rubric.3: Functional RAG with multiple vector stores, common document loaders, configurable chunking
rubric.5: Multi-modal RAG, graph knowledge, real-time data fusion, terabyte-scale with sub-second retrieval

dimensions.name: Governance
dimensions.description: Quality Control — how agents are trusted, audited, and controlled. Evaluates kill switches, guardrails, audit trails, compliance certifications, RBAC, and explainability. This is the #1 enterprise adoption blocker — platforms that nail governance win enterprise deals.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.description: Immediate halt capability, behavioral boundaries, action limits, input/output filtering, safety policies.

rubric.1: No kill switch or guardrails. Agents run unconstrained
rubric.3: Per-agent kill switch. Basic guardrails: input validation, output filtering, action limits
rubric.5: Multi-level kill switches, real-time behavioral monitoring, ML-powered guardrails, circuit breakers

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.description: Tamper-proof execution logs, regulatory compliance (SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP), data retention.

rubric.1: Minimal logging. No compliance certifications. Modifiable logs
rubric.3: Comprehensive audit trail, immutable storage, SOC2 in progress, queryable logs
rubric.5: Tamper-proof with cryptographic verification, multi-jurisdiction compliance, privacy-preserving audit

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.description: Platform access governance: custom roles, team hierarchy, SSO, least-privilege enforcement.

rubric.1: No access control. Single shared admin account
rubric.3: Role-based access with customizable roles, team support, SSO integration
rubric.5: ABAC layered on RBAC, dynamic permissions, automated reviews, privileged access management

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.description: Can stakeholders understand WHY an agent made a decision? Chain-of-thought visibility, reasoning traces, decision rationale.

rubric.1: Black box. No visibility into agent reasoning
rubric.3: Trace logs show reasoning steps and tool calls. Technical teams can follow the chain
rubric.5: Plain-language explanations, confidence scores, source citations, non-technical stakeholder reporting

dimensions.name: Operations
dimensions.description: Factory Management — production readiness and ecosystem maturity. Evaluates deployment flexibility, scaling, monitoring, cost management, and the broader ecosystem (community, funding, stability, innovation). Enterprise buyers need confidence the factory will run reliably for years.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.description: SaaS, self-hosted, hybrid, Kubernetes-native, air-gapped. Range of deployment options for enterprise requirements.

rubric.1: Single deployment mode only. No container support
rubric.3: Both cloud and self-hosted. Docker images. Basic K8s manifests
rubric.5: Full spectrum: managed SaaS multi-region, self-hosted Helm, K8s-native, serverless, FedRAMP support

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.description: Auto-scaling, concurrent agents, production dashboards, alerting, distributed tracing.

rubric.1: No scaling beyond single instance. No monitoring
rubric.3: Auto-scaling, dozens of concurrent agents. Built-in dashboard with key metrics and basic alerting
rubric.5: Hyperscale with thousands of agents, ML-powered anomaly detection, predictive alerting, enterprise observability

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.description: Token/infra cost tracking, budget alerts, optimization recommendations, chargeback for multi-team.

rubric.1: No cost tracking. Manually calculate from LLM provider bills
rubric.3: Token/cost tracking per agent with trends. Budget alerts. Basic cost breakdown
rubric.5: Real-time tracking, predictive modeling, automated model selection, ROI tracking, chargeback

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.description: Community size, enterprise adoption, financial stability, pricing transparency, innovation velocity.

rubric.1: Minimal community. Unfunded or high abandonment risk. No enterprise customers
rubric.3: Growing community (5K+ stars or equivalent). Series A+. 10+ enterprise customers. Monthly releases
rubric.5: Industry-defining. Major tech backing or profitable. 200+ enterprise customers. Standards contributor
